name: Wet from Olmedo – circular by Matapozuelos
name_original: Mojados desde Olmedo – circular por Matapozuelos
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Olmedo traverse the flat to gently rolling plains of the Castilian plateau, characterized by open agricultural landscapes and river valleys. The region features minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for sustained road cycling. Routes often connect small towns and villages, offering a blend of rural scenery and local points of interest. Best road cycling routes around Olmedo The most popular road cycling route is Wet from Olmedo – circular by Matapozuelos, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the area around Matapozuelos with gentle gradients. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Olmedo from Ataquines – circular of the Río Adaja, an easy 23.4 miles (37.6 km) path. This route follows the course of the Río Adaja, offering scenic views of the river valley. Local road cyclists also love the Íscar, Olmedo, and Mojados Loop, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail leading through multiple towns, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Road cycling around Olmedo is defined by open plains, agricultural fields, and riverine landscapes, with routes offering options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Olmedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Olmedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Olmedo?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Olmedo, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Olmedo?
A: Road cycling routes around Olmedo primarily traverse the flat to gently rolling plains of the Castilian plateau. You'll encounter open agricultural landscapes and river valleys, with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for sustained cycling.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Olmedo?
A: Yes, Olmedo offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153308064/olmedo-from-ataquines-circular-of-the-rio-adaja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmedo from Ataquines – circular of the Río Adaja&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 23.4-mile path that follows the scenic river valley.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists around Olmedo?
A: For more experienced cyclists, there are moderate routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geria Road – Simancas Castle loop from Matapozuelos&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, moderate route covering nearly 61 miles (98 km) with over 500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Olmedo?
A: The road cycling routes in Olmedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. Over 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the open plains and varied terrain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Olmedo?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Olmedo are circular. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170266290/wet-from-olmedo-circular-by-matapozuelos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wet from Olmedo – circular by Matapozuelos&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.5-mile loop exploring the area with gentle gradients.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the Olmedo area?
A: For longer rides, consider routes that connect multiple towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geria Road – Simancas Castle loop from Matapozuelos&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 61-mile (98 km) route, offering a full day's ride through the region.

Q: What interesting landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Olmedo?
A: As you cycle, you can explore various historical and cultural landmarks. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3505363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Olmedo&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5780020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Church in Mojados&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes connect these points of interest.

Q: Are there routes that pass through multiple towns, offering opportunities for cafe stops?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170286431/iscar-olmedo-and-mojados-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Íscar, Olmedo, and Mojados Loop&lt;/a&gt; connect several towns, providing excellent opportunities to stop for refreshments or explore local amenities.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Olmedo?
A: The best time for road cycling in Olmedo is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the weather is generally pleasant. Summers can be quite hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round.

Q: Are there any easy, shorter routes ideal for a quick ride?
A: Yes, for a shorter, easy ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Church, Mojados – Church of San Juan de Mojados loop from Mojados&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, covering about 11.9 miles (19 km) and taking less than an hour to complete.
tips:
Valladolid town where we find two beautiful churches built by mixing brick and masonry.
The Church of San Juan, Mudejar from the 13th and 14th centuries, in which its beautiful tower stands out, and the doorway with a pointed horseshoe arch.
The other church, very close to the path of the Cega river, is that of Santa María, Mudejar and from the 14th century.

Catholic religious sanctuary in the town of Matapozuelos, representing one of the most important examples of 15th-century architecture in Valladolid. It is also known as "La Giralda de Castilla" for its beautiful and tall tower.

Once a walled city, Olmedo retains some of its medieval charm, dating back to the Castilian and Leonese Mudejar period with several churches and heritage buildings located near the Plaza Mayor. It is a good place to stop for a coffee and admire the history.
